title: Get the tile ready for eye for Alexandria — farewell prep
html:
TL;DR

  • Five-line pre-departure todo from July 2019: get a tile for the eye for Alexandria, pack shit, have a farewell to Alexandria, get her contacts in tact, make it count, text parents good morning.
  • The "tile" reference points to Gtano's — Yahir's dad's tile business is the source supply for a personal-keepsake gift for Alexandria — the family business gets repurposed as a romance-gesture supply chain.
  • Documents the farewell-as-ritual — Yahir is leaving Phoenix or Portland after the summer with Alexandria, and the to-do list is an emotional-logistics manifest as much as a packing list.
  • "Make it count" is the directive — the same imperative that drives the Sept 10 in flight letter to self ("advance in this life and world") two months later.
  • Earliest second mention of Gtano's in the corpus — the family business as material substrate for personal life, not just a paycheck.

Notes

The note is functional but structurally revealing. "Get the tile ready for eye for Alexandria" — the typo (for eye probably meant for I or for the eye) is teen-Yahir on his phone late, but the gesture survives the typo: he's making her a piece-of-tile keepsake from his dad's stock as a parting gift. This is the same Yahir who, in 2021 (2021 05 04 turkey egypt bucket list), commissions Megan to make art for Brenda — the commissioned-keepsake-as-thanks pattern.

"Get her contacts in tact" — preserve the contact info before the inevitable phone-disorganization-loss. Practical romance.

The farewell here likely sets up the in flight Phoenix to Portland letter in September — "Remember Alexandria, because she was special, but don't get trapped or glued or even stuck on her, advance." The arc: tile-as-token (July) → physical separation → letter-to-self (September).

Privacy note

Alexandria is already in the entity graph; no new redactions.

Connections

  • 2019 09 10 low key writing this to end — September letter-to-self continuation

  • grants pass teen era
